Airline in west and central Africa

**ASKY Airlines** is a private multinational passenger [airline](/wiki/Airline "Airline") serving West and Central Africa, with its head office in [Lomé](/wiki/Lom%C3%A9 "Lomé"), [Togo](/wiki/Togo "Togo"), and its hub at [Lomé–Tokoin International Airport](/wiki/Lom%C3%A9%E2%80%93Tokoin_International_Airport "Lomé–Tokoin International Airport").[[6]](#cite_note-ET1-6)

The airline is a strategic partner of [Ethiopian Airlines](/wiki/Ethiopian_Airlines "Ethiopian Airlines"), and has been consistently profitable since 2017.[[7]](#cite_note-AfricaReport-7) It has been awarded the title of 'Best Airline in West Africa 2023' at the seventh Accraweizo event held in Accra, Ghana. The airline was recognized for its outstanding contributions to the aviation sector in the region.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asky_Airlines&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Foundation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asky_Airlines&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Foundation")]

After the pan-African airline [Air Afrique](/wiki/Air_Afrique "Air Afrique") went bankrupt in 2002, cross-border air transport in Africa became more difficult, especially in West and Central Africa. At a conference of the [Economic Community of West African States](/wiki/Economic_Community_of_West_African_States "Economic Community of West African States") (ECOWAS) and the [West African Economic and Monetary Union](/wiki/Economic_Community_of_West_African_States#West_African_Economic_and_Monetary_Union "Economic Community of West African States") (UEMOA) at [Niamey](/wiki/Niamey "Niamey") in Niger on 10 January 2004, it was decided to create a private, competitive, cost-effective airline offering all guarantees of safety and security for the region.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

In September 2005, under the initiative of [Gervais Koffi G. Djondo](/wiki/Gervais_Djondo "Gervais Djondo"), the company for the promotion of a regional airline (SPCAR) was set up, which led to various feasibility studies and market studies, and sought financial and strategic partners; this led to the establishment of ASKY Airlines in November 2007 with Gervais Koffi G. Djondo as President. On 17 January 2008, the General Meeting to establish the new international private airline was held in [Ouagadougou](/wiki/Ouagadougou "Ouagadougou"), Burkina Faso. 80% of shares were to be held by private investors, and 20% by public financial institutions whose mission is to support privately owned development institutions.[[10]](#cite_note-10) [Ethiopian Airlines](/wiki/Ethiopian_Airlines "Ethiopian Airlines") became the technical and strategic partner under a management contract for the first five years of operation, holding a 40% stake.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

Originally planned for April 2009, the first revenue flight took place on 15 January 2010.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 2020 ASKY Airlines was found to be operating the world's shortest international flight route, between [Kinshasa](/wiki/N%27djili_Airport "N'djili Airport") and [Brazzaville](/wiki/Maya-Maya_Airport "Maya-Maya Airport"), at 13 miles (21 km).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Corporate affairs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asky_Airlines&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Corporate affairs")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9f/ETAOK-DGAA.jpg/250px-ETAOK-DGAA.jpg)](/wiki/File:ETAOK-DGAA.jpg)

An Asky Airlines [Boeing 737-700](/wiki/Boeing_737-700 "Boeing 737-700") at [Accra International Airport](/wiki/Accra_International_Airport "Accra International Airport"), [Accra](/wiki/Accra "Accra"), [Ghana](/wiki/Ghana "Ghana") in 2019.

### Ownership

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asky_Airlines&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ownership")]

The airline is privately owned. Main shareholders are [Ethiopian Airlines](/wiki/Ethiopian_Airlines "Ethiopian Airlines") (40%), [Ecobank](/wiki/Ecobank "Ecobank"), BIDC, [BOAD](/wiki/West_African_Development_Bank "West African Development Bank"), Sakhumnotho Group Holding, and other West and Central African private investors as well as the Togolese government having a minority stake of 14.39%.[[13]](#cite_note-AFRAA2019-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

### Business trends

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asky_Airlines&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Business trends")]

Asky Airlines has been reported as being profitable,[[15]](#cite_note-15) although accounts do not seem to have been published. The airline states that it first registered a profit in 2015 and has been consistently profitable since 2017.[[7]](#cite_note-AfricaReport-7) In the 2021 fiscal year, Asky made a profit of over 12 million dollars, and in 2022, it made a profit of 30 million dollars.[[16]](#cite_note-16) The airline has carried over 1.2 million passengers in 2019 alone with 15 aircraft serving 28 destinations across west, central, south and eastern Africa with a plan to expand to north Africa.[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)

## Destinations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asky_Airlines&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Destinations")]

As of 2023, Asky airlines serves 28 destinations in 26 countries within Africa, focusing on developing a strong intra-Africa network that fosters regional development, tourism, economic growth, and regional integration as a major economic catalyst within the continent with its long-term goal of a sustainable business focused on profitability.[[22]](#cite_note-22) The Airline served the following 19 scheduled destinations throughout West and Central Africa from its hub at Lome until October 2017.[[23]](#cite_note-Network-23)

### Alliances and codeshare agreements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asky_Airlines&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Alliances and codeshare agreements")]

In 2021, ASKY became a member of [IATA](/wiki/IATA "IATA").[[26]](#cite_note-26)

Asky Airlines have [Codeshare agreements](/wiki/Codeshare_agreements "Codeshare agreements") with the following airlines:

* [Air Senegal](/wiki/Air_Senegal "Air Senegal")[[27]](#cite_note-27)
* [Ethiopian Airlines](/wiki/Ethiopian_Airlines "Ethiopian Airlines")

## Accidents and incidents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Asky_Airlines&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Accidents and incidents")]

* On 10 January 2015 an Asky Airlines [Boeing 737-43QSF](/wiki/Boeing_737_Classic "Boeing 737 Classic") (leased from [Ethiopian Airlines](/wiki/Ethiopian_Airlines "Ethiopian Airlines")), was damaged beyond repair in a landing accident and runway excursion at [Accra International Airport](/wiki/Accra_International_Airport "Accra International Airport"), [Accra](/wiki/Accra "Accra"), [Ghana](/wiki/Ghana "Ghana"). The aircraft was written off but there were no fatalities.[[29]](#cite_note-29)
